documents.title: bpftime roadmap and ideas for 2024
documents.description: 1. 让 Userspace eBPF 和内核的 eBPF 协同工作，完成复杂任务，并且和 Kernel 的 eBPF 机制完全兼容
documents.titleText: bpftime roadmap and ideas for 2024
documents.descriptionText: 1. 让 userspace ebpf 和内核的 ebpf 协同工作，完成复杂任务，并且和 kernel 的 ebpf 机制完全兼容
documents.bodyTerms: 一些接下来 bpftime 可以做的方向整理： 1. userspace ebpf 和内核的 协同工作，完成复杂任务，并且和 kernel 机制完全兼容 完善功能以无缝支持大型 应用程序。当前的大型 程序都需要内核态和用户态协同工作，把内核态和用户态的信息通过 maps 和读取相互关联起来，这也是和传统的 dbi 框架的不同之处。 我们将提供两种模式： 仅在用户空间运行 ebpf（模式 1） 可在非 linux 系统上无需内核地运行 对于大型 应用并不非常适用，因为在共享内存中的映射无法被内核的 程序所使用 2. 在用户空间与内核空间同时运行 ebpf，并配备 daemon 更好地与内核 兼容，支持包含数千行 代码的大型 应用，同时支持 uprobe、kprobe、socket 等混合使用 让内核态的 与用户态的 协同工作 无需修改内核，但会改变 bpf 系统调用的行为 这一思路与 fuse 有相似之处，需要一个内核中的 程序和用户态的 协同工作。 相结合的新的用户态和内核态交互的方式、编程模型 随着计算领域的不断进步，我们所面临的技术问题也变得越来越复杂。其中，如何减少内核与用户空间的交互开销，并提供更高效、更灵活的编程模型，成为了研究的热点。本文将深入探讨 ebpf（extended berkeley packet filter）如何为这一问题带来了新的解决方案。 现有的内核与用户空间交互策略 首先，我们需要认识到，上下文切换——尤其是在同步的系统调用中——确实会带来相当大的性能开销。为了优化这种交互，开发者和研究者们提出了以下几种策略： iouring ：这是一个新的轻量级的异步i/o接口，旨在减少上下文切换，从而提高i/o操作的效率。iouring ring buffer 实现了用户空间和内核空间的双向数据传输，从而避免了传统的系统调用开销。 系统调用批处理 ：这种策略会将多个系统调用合并为一个批次执行，从而减少上下文切换的次数。 异步系统调用 ：与传统的同步系统调用不同，异步调用不需要等待其完成，从而可以继续执行其他任务。一般来说它的原理是：用户空间发起一个系统调用，内核立即返回，然后在后台执行这个系统调用，最后通知用户空间系统调用已经完成。 这些方法虽然取得了一定的成功，但在面对现代计算的复杂环境时，仍存在一些局限性。 ebpf：新的交互方式 ebpf，一种在内核中运行的程序，提供了一种全新的机制来处理和优化用户空间和内核之间的交互。以下是 所带来的几个关键特性： ：ebpf 是一种特殊的数据结构，它们可以在用户空间和内核之间无缝地工作，极大地减少了数据交换的系统调用开销。 mmap ：这个特性允许开发者将 映射到用户空间和内核之间的共享内存，从而实现高效的数据访问。 user 类似，这两种 map 提供了一种高效的双向数据传输机制，进一步减少了系统调用的需要。 动态代码修改 的另一个显著特点是它可以在运行时修改和优化内核或用户空间的代码。这为开发者提供了一个强大的工具，使他们能够在不修改源代码或重新启动系统的情况下进行微调。 协同执行 允许部分功能在用户空间执行，部分在内核空间执行，两者可以协同工作，并异步调用。 pass 和用户空间协同工作，可以跳过一部分内核中传统的较长的处理路径，从而提高性能。例如，xdp 实现了一个高效的网络数据包处理路径，它可以在内核中跳过传统的网络协议栈，直接将数据包传递给用户空间的程序进行处理。 ebpf，作为一个相对较新的技术，已经展现出其强大的潜力，为内核与用户空间的交互带来了新的视角和工具。而它的真正价值可能还在继续发掘之中。不过，正如任何技术工具，它不是银弹，最终如何使用仍取决于具体的应用场景和需求。不过，毫无疑问，ebpf为我们提供了一个探索更高效、更灵活内核与用户态交互方式的宝贵机会。 3. fuse: 探索用户态文件系统和内核态相结合并协同加速的可能性 在现代操作系统中，fuse（用户空间文件系统）已经成为了一个受欢迎的选择，允许开发者在用户空间创建文件系统，而无需对内核代码进行修改。但是，系统调用的成本仍然存在。这是bpftime能够发挥作用的地方。 核心优势与可能性 用户态和内核态协同优化 通过bpftime，可以在用户空间预先处理某些文件系统操作，如缓存、元数据查询等，并仅在真正需要的时候与内核空间的ebpf交互。这样，可以极大地减少不必要的系统调用，提高文件操作的速度，加速一些可能的快速路径，类似 xrp 的机制一样。 用户态 bypass 我们可以做的完全 fuse，使用 将对应的用户态程序和 vfs 、libfuse 对接起来，实现完全的 用户态文件系统，并且可以类似 那样，不需要对用户态应用进行侵入式改变或链接。 动态调整策略 用户态的bpftime可以动态地收集性能数据，并据此调整策略，例如选择最佳的缓存策略，而这些都可以在运行时进行，无需停机或重启。 4. 更强的自定义能力 开发者可以根据具体的应用场景，定制特定的ebpf程序，以适应不同的工作负载和优化目标。 network cilium: 为网络通信带来协同智能 网络是现代计算环境中的基础，但随着越来越复杂的通信模式和高流量的需求，传统的网络处理方式可能无法满足现代应用的需求。在此背景下，bpftime与网络库的结合为我们提供了一种新的方法来处理网络请求。 智能网络流处理 bpftime允许开发者在用户空间预处理网络请求，例如：流量平衡、qos策略等，并与内核空间的ebpf进行协同，只有在真正需要的时候进行系统调用。这样，可以有效减少上下文切换，提高网络效率。 动态网络策略调整 与文件系统协同工作相似，bpftime可以动态地收集网络性能数据，并根据这些数据调整网络处理策略，例如：动态调整流量分配、快速切换通信路径等。 更高的网络吞吐量 通过在用户空间预处理网络请求，可以避免传统网络堆栈的开销，从而实现更高的网络吞吐量，特别是在高流量和低延迟的场景中。可以将用户态 和用户态的网络库结合起来，在类似 cilium 的场景中加速 service mesh 的网络请求转发和处理。 bpftime为用户空间和内核空间的协同工作开辟了新的可能性，无论是在文件系统还是网络通信方面。这种协同优化为我们提供了一个高效、灵活并且可自定义的框架，可以满足现代复杂计算环境的需求。未来，这种协同工作模式有可能成为操作系统和应用之间交互的标准方式。 5.

documents.bodyTerms: extended berkeley packet filter (ebpf) and webassembly (wasm) are emerging powerful technologies for enhancing system capabilities cloud native environments. however, deploying ebpf programs scale presents challenges. this blog explores how integrating with runtimes like wasmedge addresses these challenges enabling lightweight, portable, secure execution workloads. the integration streamlines deployment kubernetes through wasm's pluggable model while improving security fine grained access controls. examples also demonstrate enhanced observability, debugging control wasm wasm. introduction that playing important roles modern ecosystems. post can combined enhance observability innovative ways. provides yet lightweight framework running sandboxed securely within linux kernel. enables dynamic programmable extensions tasks networking, tracing, security. comes its own set around permissions, lifecycle management, pluggability. webassembly, other hand, designed portability, security, high performance. interfaces wasi well suited applications plugin systems. discusses bpf address

documents.titleText: nvbit 内部机制深度分析
documents.descriptionText: 本文档通过源码分析、二进制逆向、strace/ltrace/gdb 运行时追踪，全面剖析 nvbit v1.7.6 的内部工作原理。 适合希望理解 nvbit 底层实现并进行扩展开发的读者。
documents.bodyTerms: 本文档通过源码分析、二进制逆向、strace/ltrace/gdb 运行时追踪，全面剖析 nvbit v1.7.6 的内部工作原理。 适合希望理解 底层实现并进行扩展开发的读者。 1. 整体架构 2. 注入机制：从加载到拦截 3. cuda api 拦截原理 4. sass 反汇编流水线 5. 二进制打补丁引擎 6. 寄存器保存与恢复 7. gpu cpu 通信机制 8. elf/cubin 9. 硬件抽象层 (hal) 10. libnvbit.a 内部结构 11. .so 的二进制结构 12. 完整运行时序 13. 14. 扩展开发指南 15. strace 实证分析 16. 使用非 语言编写 17. poc：手写 ptx 设备函数 的架构分为四个层次： ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ 用户工具层 (tool) instr count.cu inject funcs.cu makefile init, event 等回调 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ nvbit.h: get instrs, insert call, ... reg rw.h: read reg, write utils/channel.hpp: channeldev, channelhost 核心引擎 (libnvbit.a) 单例类 回调分发、模块管理、函数追踪 function 反汇编、代码生成、二进制打补丁 sassinstr 指令编解码 elf 处理库 cubin 解析、符号表、重定位 gk11x hal (kepler) gm10x (maxwell) gv10x (volta) gv11x tu10x (turing) ga10x (ampere) gh10x (hopper) gb10x (blackwell sm100) gb12x (sm120) └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ 1.1 内部模块 静态库 24 个目标文件 ，按功能可分为： 目标文件 代码大小 imp.o 239kb text 372kb data 主实现 预编译 辅助内核 代码补丁

documents.description: Tutorial Github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial Official NVBit repo: <https://github.com/NVlabs/NVBit
documents.titleText: nvbit tutorial: comprehensive guide to gpu binary instrumentation
documents.descriptionText: tutorial github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial official nvbit repo: <https://github.com/nvlabs/nvbit
documents.bodyTerms: tutorial github repo: official nvbit this repository provides comprehensive, blog style (unofficial) for learning nvidia binary instrumentation tool (nvbit). offers detailed, step guidance with depth explanations code help you understand gpu concepts and techniques. covered the same end user license agreement that cuda toolkit. using agree described eula.txt file. table contents quick start (5 minutes) about prerequisites introduction requirements building tools key creating your own faq troubleshooting contributing get started just few minutes: ensure toolkit installed nvdisasm path export path=/usr/local/cuda/bin:$path clone (if haven't already) build all make test applications apps run first (instruction counting) preload=./tools/instr count/instr count.so ./test apps/vectoradd/vectoradd should see output like: kernel 0 vecadd(...) thread blocks 98, instructions 50077, total 50077 next steps: read instr count tutorial, try opcode hist analyze instruction mix, encounter issues. goes

documents.title: NVBit Tutorial: Basic Block Instruction Counting
documents.description: Github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.titleText: nvbit tutorial: basic block instruction counting
documents.descriptionText: github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.bodyTerms: github repo: tl;dr: like instr count but 10 50x faster. use this for production profiling. quick start: env cuda injection64 path=./tools/instr bb/instr bb.so ./test apps/vectoradd/vectoradd output: kernel 0 z6vecaddpds thread blocks 98, instructions 62588, total 62588 overview basic block sequence with single entry point (the first instruction) and exit last instruction). branches exist the middle block, target block. property makes ideal efficient instrumentation. instead instrumenting every instruction (as tool), identifies all function, instruments only each passes size instrumentation updates counter based entire size. approach drastically reduces number points function calls during execution while still providing accurate count. code structure count.cu host that analyzes identify blocks, tracks predicated separately (optional), reports results after inject funcs.cu device provides instrs (increments size) pred off (tracks instructions) how works: side (instr count.cu)

documents.titleText: nvbit tutorial: instruction counting with cuda graphs support
documents.descriptionText: github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.bodyTerms: github repo: tl;dr: like instr count but works with cuda graphs. use this your app uses cudagraphlaunch, stream capture, manual graph construction. quick start: env injection64 path=./tools/instr graph/instr graph.so ./test apps/vectoradd/vectoradd both traditional and based launches what are graphs? graphs let you record sequences kernel replay them efficiently. think "recording macro" gpu operations for faster execution. (works regular count) (int 0; (); (needs graph) cudagraph graph; cudastreambegincapture(stream); cudastreamendcapture(stream, &graph); map; managed uint64 counter[max num kernel]; unlike the basic instruction counter which single counter, tool: 1. maps each function unique 2. allocates separate memory 3. tracks max different kernels approach allows multiple update their counters concurrently without interference. launch instrumentation void try instrument(cufunction cucontext ctx) skip encountered (kernel map.find(f) map.end()) return; stop instrumenting run out kernel) keep total number

documents.descriptionText: github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.bodyTerms: github repo: tl;dr: this tool counts how many gpu instructions your cuda kernels execute. perfect for learning nvbit basics. quick start: env injection64 path=./tools/instr count/instr count.so ./test apps/vectoradd/vectoradd output: kernel 0 z6vecaddpds thread blocks 98, 62588, total 62588 table contents overview what are predicates? architecture diagram code structure works: host side device building the running sample output common issues next steps instruction counting demonstrates fundamental workflow instrumentation: intercept launches, analyze and instrument before execution, collect data during process report results after completion. tutorial walks through implementation detail help you understand build similar tools. diving into code, key concept: predicates can conditionally executed using predicates: @p0 add r1, r2, r3 only executes predicate p0 true @!p1 mov r4, r5 p1 false think like statements level. when false, "predicated off"

documents.descriptionText: github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.bodyTerms: github repo: known issue: this tool has device function invocation issues the current implementation. it's included educational example gpu cpu communication concepts. tl;dr: demonstrates sending printf style messages from cpu. showing inter patterns. status: partially functional channel works, but calls may fail. see main readme faq for details. comparison: nvbit cuda feature mem printf2 requires source code (binary instrumentation) yes can add any binary selective instrumentation (by kernel, instruction) performance overhead high (50 200x) medium (5 20x) setup complexity complex (channel, threads) simple (builtin) best learning, analysis normal development when use instead: you have code, just this: global void kernel() printf("thread value %f\n", threadidx.x, value); useful: analyzing third party binaries without adding specific instructions learning patterns research overview provides simplified like functionality code. traditional debugging methods side debuggers

documents.title: NVBit Tutorial: Memory Tracing
documents.description: Github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.titleText: nvbit tutorial: memory tracing
documents.descriptionText: github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.bodyTerms: github repo: tl;dr: captures every memory access (addresses, warp, cta). essential for finding coalescing issues and patterns. very high overhead use selectively! quick start: warning: overhead! instrument only first kernel env start grid num=0 end num=1 cuda injection64 path=./tools/mem trace/mem trace.so ./test apps/vectoradd/vectoradd trace.txt typical output: memtrace: ctx 0x... launch 0 cta 0,0,0 warp ldg.e.64 0x710c9b06ba00 0x710c9b06ba08 0x710c9b06ba10 ... (32 addresses, 8 byte stride) overview understanding patterns crucial optimizing gpu code. the mem trace tool instruments all instructions kernel, addresses accessed each collects contextual information (grid, block, ids), sends this data efficiently from cpu, prints analyzes trace. enables developers identify patterns, detect issues, find divergence, optimize layouts. code structure trace.cu host side that establishes communication channel between identifies instructions, processes inject funcs.cu device threads packages with execution context,

documents.descriptionText: github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.bodyTerms: github repo: warning: research/educational tool only this has 100 1000x overhead. use for: learning instruction replacement concepts research (fault injection, custom operations) small scale testing not for production performance profiling! tl;dr: replaces mov instructions with function calls. demonstrates how modify gpu code runtime. overview the replace intercepts all (move) cuda kernel and them calls device that performs equivalent operation. several advanced capabilities nvbit: removing original instructions, reading writing registers directly, handling different operand types (immediate values, registers, uniform registers), preserving semantics instruction. while specific example simply replicates functionality, same approach can extended add logging/profiling implement versions operations debugging, simulate architectural features present hardware, test alternative implementations operations. structure replace.cu host side identifies analyzes types, removes inserts inject funcs.cu implements function, uses register access intrinsics read/write handles various works:

documents.descriptionText: github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.bodyTerms: github repo: tl;dr: shows what types instructions your kernels execute (loads, stores, math, etc.). essential for understanding kernel behavior. quick start: env cuda injection64 path=./tools/opcode hist/opcode hist.so ./test apps/vectoradd/vectoradd output instruction histogram: dadd 3125 exit 6261 imad 3136 ldg.e.64 6250 stg.e.64 overview the opcode histogram tool extends basic concepts from counting example but adds ability categorize their (operation code). this provides insight into which dominate kernel's execution, helping you focus optimization efforts. example, might discover: high percentage memory operations, suggesting bound code many type conversions, indicating potential data mismatches excessive synchronization that could optimized structure hist.cu host maps opcodes unique ids, inserts instrumentation each instruction, and aggregates prints after execution inject funcs.cu device executes gpu updates counters managed how works: side (opcode hist.cu) let's examine key elements implementation:

documents.title: NVBit Tutorial Tools
documents.description: This directory contains example instrumentation tools built with NVBit (NVIDIA Binary Instrumentation Tool). Each tool demonstrates different capabilities and serves as a starting point for developing your own instrument
documents.titleText: nvbit tutorial tools
documents.descriptionText: this directory contains example instrumentation tools built with nvbit (nvidia binary instrumentation tool). each tool demonstrates different capabilities and serves as a starting point for developing your own instrument
documents.bodyTerms: this directory contains example instrumentation tools built with nvbit (nvidia binary tool). each tool demonstrates different capabilities and serves starting point for developing your own tools. overview instr count basic instruction counting opcode hist histogram executed instructions record reg vals records register values during execution block level cuda graph mem printf2 memory access printing trace collection mov replace replacement common structure typically consists host side code (.cu file the same name tool) that sets instrumentation, manages callbacks, processes results; device (usually inject funcs.cu) functions injected into gpu code; makefile compiles shared library (.so); readme.md documentation specific tool. running alternatively: injection64 path=/path/to/tool.so ./your application most support configuration through environment variables. check tool's readme options. test repository includes simple vector addition /test apps/vectoradd/ can used these tools: compile ../test apps/vectoradd

documents.titleText: nvbit tutorial: register value recording
documents.descriptionText: github repo: <https://github.com/eunomia-bpf/nvbit-tutorial
documents.bodyTerms: github repo: tl;dr: captures register values during kernel execution. perfect for debugging numerical errors (nan/inf), algorithm verification, and understanding data flow. very high overhead. quick start: instrument specific instruction range start grid num=0 end num=1 instr begin=10 end=15 env cuda injection64 path=./tools/record reg vals/record vals.so ./test apps/vectoradd/vectoradd use cases: finding where nan/infinity appears verifying correctness divergence issues flow overview the dynamic registers execution invaluable performance analysis. record vals tool instruments selected instructions capture values, collects from all threads warp, transfers this efficiently gpu cpu, prints analyzes state information. capability allows developers debug complex examining verify through computation stages, detect value patterns anomalies, understand between warp. code structure vals.cu host side that identifies selects instrument, determines which track, processes displays inject funcs.cu device packages with context, sends channel common.h

documents.title: hXDP: Efficient Software Packet Processing on FPGA NICs
documents.description: Authors: Marco Spaziani Brunella, Giacomo Belocchi, Marco Bonola, Salvatore Pontarelli, Giuseppe Siracusano, Giuseppe Bianchi, Aniello Cammarano, Alessandro Palumbo, Luca Petrucci, Roberto Bifulco
documents.titleText: hxdp: efficient software packet processing on fpga nics
documents.descriptionText: authors: marco spaziani brunella, giacomo belocchi, marco bonola, salvatore pontarelli, giuseppe siracusano, giuseppe bianchi, aniello cammarano, alessandro palumbo, luca petrucci, roberto bifulco
documents.bodyTerms: hxdp: fpga 网卡上高效的软件包处理 authors: marco spaziani brunella, giacomo belocchi, bonola, salvatore pontarelli, giuseppe siracusano, bianchi, aniello cammarano, alessandro palumbo, luca petrucci, roberto bifulco 作者： affiliations: axbryd, cnit, university rome tor vergata, nec laboratories europe 机构： axbryd、cnit、罗马第二大学、nec 欧洲实验室 conference: 14th usenix symposium operating systems design and implementation (osdi '20) 会议： 第14届 操作系统设计与实现研讨会 abstract accelerators the nic enable offloading expensive packet processing tasks from cpu. however, fpgas have limited resources that may need shared among diverse applications, programming them difficult. 加速器能够将昂贵的数据包处理任务从 cpu 上卸载。然而，fpga 资源有限，可能需要在不同应用之间共享，并且对其编程也很困难。 present solution run linux's express data path programs written ebpf fpgas, using only fraction available hardware while matching performance high end cpus. 我们提出了一种在 上运行用 编写的 linux 程序的解决方案，仅使用一小部分可用硬件资源，同时达到高端 的性能。 iterative execution model not good fit for accelerators. nonetheless, show many instructions program can compressed, parallelized completely removed,

documents.title: hXDP: Efficient Software Packet Processing on FPGA NICs / Abstract

documents.descriptionText: 1. policy tuning 2. event monitoring and observability 3. high-speed data processing 4. access control and security 5. testing and error injection 6. patch fixing and dynamic updates
documents.bodyTerms: classification kernel ebpf use cases 1. policy tuning 2. event monitoring and observability 3. high speed data processing 4. access control security 5. testing error injection 6. patch fixing dynamic updates definition: dynamically adjusting policies optimize system performance behavior. cases: cpu scheduling: example: the scheduler’s behavior based real time load using programs prioritize critical processes, ensuring responsive for key services under conditions. controlling scheduler with bpf https://lwn.net/articles/873244/ lot usecases this year lpc: sched ext: extensible class https://lpc.events/event/18/sessions/192/ ghost (sosp 21) https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3477132.3483542 memory management optimization: modifying page reclamation strategy during pressure. can influence how reclaims pages, optimizing utilization intensive applications. lpc few papers https://lpc.events/event/18/contributions/1932/ https://arxiv.org/pdf/2409.11220 i/o policy: scheduling current disk usage patterns. instance, read/write operations database workloads ebpf. fuse: https://lwn.net/articles/915717/ https://lpc.events/event/16/contributions/1339/attachments/945/1861/lpc2022%20fuse bpf.pdf hid driver https://docs.kernel.org/hid/hid bpf.html used steamdeck fuse
